tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Replace the first occurrence of old_string with new_string in a workspace file. old_string must be unique in the file.

tools.name: glob_search
tools.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. *.go, **/*.md) under the workspace using filepath.Walk.

pattern.description: Glob pattern relative to search base

tools.name: grep_search
tools.description: Search file contents with a regular expression under the workspace (Go regexp syntax). Skips large files and common dependency directories.

tools.name: http_request
tools.description: Perform an HTTP request (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD). Supports JSON object body via json_body; otherwise raw body string. Large responses are truncated.

body.description: Raw request body (ignored if json_body set)

method.description: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD

tools.name: list_dir
tools.description: List files and subdirectories in a workspace directory (non-recursive). Append trailing separator for directories.

path.description: Directory relative to workspace root; use . for root

tools.name: list_skills
tools.description: List all available skills with names and descriptions. Use load_skill with a name to get the full skill content.

tools.name: load_skill
tools.description: Load the full content of a named skill. Skills are domain-specific knowledge modules (e.g. go-review, security-audit, api-design). Use list_skills first to discover available skill names.

name.description: Skill name as shown in list_skills output

tools.name: mcp_repo_probe
tools.description: Probe repository paths and return basic stats (mock).

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file within the workspace. Optional 1-based start_line and end_line (inclusive). Large files without line range are capped.

end_line.description: Last line to include (1-based); omit for end of file

start_line.description: First line to include (1-based); omit for start of file

tools.name: run_shell
tools.description: Run a shell command with optional working directory (relative to workspace). stdout and stderr are combined. Uses sh -c on Unix and cmd /C on Windows.

command.description: Command line to execute (shell on Unix: sh -c)

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file under the workspace root. Parent directories are created as needed.
